sources.name: CourtListener (Free Law Project)
sources.coverage: ~200 federal + state courts; SCOTUS 1754–present; oral-argument audio; judge database; RECAP PACER archive (hundreds of millions of docket/document objects)
sources.access: REST API v4 (token, free); bulk S3 (s3://com-courtlistener-storage/bulk-data/ --no-sign-request); 2TB public embeddings bucket; webhooks
sources.license: Site content CC BY-ND 4.0; underlying opinions/PACER docs are public domain (government edicts). Respect 'blocked' flag in redistribution.

sources.name: RECAP Archive
sources.coverage: Tens of millions of PACER documents, dockets, entries, parties, attorneys; mirrored to Internet Archive
sources.access: Same CourtListener API (docket, docket-entries, recap_document); bulk available via FLP
sources.license: Court-authored docs public domain; third-party filings freely shareable per FLP's established RECAP practice

sources.name: PACER
sources.coverage: All US federal district/bankruptcy/appellate dockets (>1B documents)
sources.access: Per-court CM/ECF; RSS for recent entries; NO bulk download; automated bulk harvesting forbidden by ToS
sources.license: Documents PD but access metered ($0.10/page, $3.00 cap, quarterly $30 waiver). Prefer RECAP.

sources.name: Supreme Court of the United States
sources.coverage: Slip opinions (current term), orders, argument transcripts, bound U.S. Reports (historical)
sources.access: Direct PDF/XML fetch; no API; /xmls/current and /xmls/archive for docket XML; GovInfo bulk for legacy FLITE 1937–1975

sources.name: Caselaw Access Project (Harvard LIL)
sources.coverage: ~6.7M cases / 40M pages; all officially book-published US state + federal case law through 2020 (earliest 1658)
sources.access: Bulk download at case.law/caselaw/; native case.law search disabled Sept 2024 and redirected to CourtListener; CAPAPI at api.case.law/v1/ — uncertain — verify post-2024 status. HF mirrors: TeraflopAI/Caselaw_Access_Project, free-law/Caselaw_Access_Project, harvard-lil/cold-cases
sources.format: JSON, XML, text; Parquet on HF
sources.license: CC0 1.0 (fully open since March 2024 when Ravel/LexisNexis embargo lifted)

sources.name: Cornell Legal Information Institute (LII)
sources.coverage: US Code, CFR, SCOTUS opinions (1990–present + selected historical), UCC, Federal Rules, Wex legal encyclopedia, state linkouts
sources.access: HTML scraping; no official API or bulk dump
sources.license: LII value-add (Wex, annotations) under CC BY-NC-SA (uncertain exact variant — verify at law.cornell.edu/lii/terms/documentation). Underlying law is public domain.

sources.name: GovInfo (GPO)
sources.coverage: Federal Register, CFR, Public Laws, Statutes at Large, US Code, Congressional Record/Reports/Hearings, USCOURTS, US Reports, CRS Reports, Compilation of Presidential Documents, Bills/Bill Status, and more
sources.access: api.govinfo.gov (api.data.gov key, free); bulk repository at /bulkdata (/BILLS, /CFR, /ECFR, /FR, /PLAW, /STATUTE, /USCOURTS, /COMPS)
sources.format: XML (USLM for statutes), PDF, MODS metadata, JSON

sources.name: eCFR
sources.coverage: Current editorially-updated CFR (50 titles); point-in-time and historical versions; daily updates
sources.access: Public web API (no key) at /api/; bulk XML at govinfo.gov/bulkdata/ECFR; annual CFR back to 1996 at /bulkdata/CFR
sources.license: Public domain. Note: eCFR is editorial, not the legally-authoritative annual CFR.

sources.name: Federal Register 2.0
sources.coverage: All Federal Register documents since 1994 (rules, proposed rules, notices, presidential documents)
sources.access: REST API /api/v1 (JSON/CSV, no key required); bulk XML via GovInfo

sources.name: Congress.gov API
sources.coverage: Bills, amendments, summaries, members, committee reports, nominations, treaties, Congressional Record — back to 81st Congress (1949) for many endpoints
sources.access: REST v3 with api.data.gov key; 5,000 req/hr, 250/page. For bulk prefer GovInfo /BILLS, /BILLSTATUS, /BILLSUM, /CREC, /CRPT.

sources.name: SEC EDGAR
sources.coverage: All SEC filings since 1993/94 (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, 13F, S-1, DEF 14A, 20-F, etc.); 20M+ filings
sources.access: data.sec.gov REST (submissions, company-concept, company-facts, frames); bulk ZIPs (companyfacts.zip, submissions.zip); full-text search efts.sec.gov

sources.name: Justia
sources.coverage: SCOTUS (1791–), federal appellate, federal district (partial), state courts (variable), US Code, CFR
sources.access: No public bulk API; HTML scraping violates Justia ToS at scale
sources.license: Underlying law is PD; Justia annotations may carry copyright; scraping risky

sources.name: Law Library of Congress / Congress.gov Treaty Documents
sources.coverage: Senate treaty documents and ratifications; CRS legal reports; foreign law guides
